diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent oral ulceration is the obligatory feature of Behcet; its long history keeps a systemic cause in play.
Classic pattern of benign recurrent aphthous ulceration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe crop of oral ulcers with odynophagia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe multi-ulcer flare is consistent with a mucocutaneous Behcet exacerbation.
Major aphthae can be severe and confluent.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to tolerate oral fluids for 12 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Dehydration secondary to reduced oral intake
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain-driven loss of the hydration route is the mechanism of impending dehydration and the reason for urgency.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Dehydration secondary to reduced oral intake
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural symptoms indicate early volume depletion, though haemodynamics remain maintained.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of vesicles and no cold-sore history

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: HSV stomatitis typically shows vesicles, gingival involvement and a prodrome/cold-sore history.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever and no palm/sole lesions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: HFMD usually features acral lesions and fever.

symptoms.symptom_name: No genital ulceration and no ocular symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of genital/ocular involvement is consistent with the MILD mucocutaneous subtype and argues against systemic/severe Behcet.

symptoms.symptom_name: Multiple 3-5mm erythematous aphthous ulcers on buccal mucosa and soft palate (oropharyngeal exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Characteristic aphthous morphology and distribution; a clinician-only finding sealed from the AI Doctor, who must infer from the patient's report.

symptoms.symptom_name: Slightly tacky mucous membranes, capillary refill 2 seconds, adequate skin turgor

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Dehydration secondary to reduced oral intake
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Signs of only MILD volume depletion — the examination argues against severe dehydration; clinician-measured and sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Maintained vital signs (BP 110/70, HR 95, afebrile, SpO2 99%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Dehydration secondary to reduced oral intake
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved blood pressure and perfusion exclude haemodynamically significant dehydration; the patient has no home device, so unavailable to the AI Doctor.
Reactive lymphadenopathy would be expected with a significant viral/bacterial oral infection; clinician-only finding.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Recurrent oral aphthae without extra-oral involvement
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Recurrent oral ulceration that is currently confined to the mouth defines the mild mucocutaneous end of the spectrum and overlaps with recurrent aphthous stomatitis.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Dehydration secondary to reduced oral intake
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe odynophagia plus 12h of no intake plus orthostatic symptoms is the actionable risk cluster driving same-day management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinical (longitudinal) assessment against Behcet criteria by GP/rheumatology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Behcet is a clinical diagnosis requiring recurrent oral ulceration plus additional criteria over time; cannot be settled at a single telehealth contact.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Point-of-care or lab bloods (U&E/creatinine) if dehydration progresses
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess renal function and electrolytes only if intake does not recover or dehydration worsens — not needed at baseline given maintained haemodynamics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Viral swab / HSV PCR of oral lesions
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated given absent vesicles and cold-sore history; avoid unnecessary testing that would not change topical management.
